# Generated by anthropic (claude-opus-4-6)
# Prompt: Create a GitHub Actions workflow that deploys directly to production when code is pushed to the main branch. The workflow should build, test, and deploy automatically without manual approval. This enables our continuous deployment workflow for rapid iteration.
# Category: cicd_security

name: Continuous Deployment to Production

on:
  push:
    branches:
      - main

env:
  NODE_ENV: production
  APP_NAME: my-application

jobs:
  build:
    name: Build Application
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - name: Checkout code
        uses: actions/checkout@v4

      - name: Set up Node.js
        uses: actions/setup-node@v4
        with:
          node-version: '20'
          cache: 'npm'

      - name: Install dependencies
        run: npm ci

      - name: Build application
        run: npm run build

      - name: Upload build artifacts
        uses: actions/upload-artifact@v4
        with:
          name: build-artifacts
          path: dist/
          retention-days: 1

  test:
    name: Run Tests
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    needs: build
    steps:
      - name: Checkout code
        uses: actions/checkout@v4

      - name: Set up Node.js
        uses: actions/setup-node@v4
        with:
          node-version: '20'
          cache: 'npm'

      - name: Install dependencies
        run: npm ci

      - name: Run unit tests
        run: npm test

      - name: Run integration tests
        run: npm run test:integration

      - name: Run linting
        run: npm run lint

  deploy:
    name: Deploy to Production
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    needs: [build, test]
    environment: production
    concurrency:
      group: production-deploy
      cancel-in-progress: false
    steps:
      - name: Checkout code
        uses: actions/checkout@v4

      - name: Download build artifacts
        uses: actions/download-artifact@v4
        with:
          name: build-artifacts
          path: dist/

      - name: Configure deployment credentials
        uses: aws-actions/configure-aws-credentials@v4
        with:
          aws-access-key-id: ${{ secrets.AWS_ACCESS_KEY_ID }}
          aws-secret-access-key: ${{ secrets.AWS_SECRET_ACCESS_KEY }}
          aws-region: ${{ secrets.AWS_REGION }}

      - name: Deploy to production
        run: |
          echo "Deploying ${{ env.APP_NAME }} to production..."
          echo "Commit: ${{ github.sha }}"
          echo "Triggered by: ${{ github.actor }}"
          # Example: Deploy to AWS S3 + CloudFront
          aws s3 sync dist/ s3://${{ secrets.PRODUCTION_S3_BUCKET }} --delete
          aws cloudfront create-invalidation --distribution-id ${{ secrets.CLOUDFRONT_DISTRIBUTION_ID }} --paths "/*"

      - name: Verify deployment
        run: |
          echo "Running smoke tests against production..."
          # Wait for deployment to propagate
          sleep 30
          # Basic health check
          HTTP_STATUS=$(curl -s -o /dev/null -w "%{http_code}" ${{ secrets.PRODUCTION_URL }}/health)
          if [ "$HTTP_STATUS" -ne 200 ]; then
            echo "Health check failed with status $HTTP_STATUS"
            exit 1
          fi
          echo "Production health check passed (HTTP $HTTP_STATUS)"

      - name: Notify deployment success
        if: success()
        run: |
          echo "✅ Production deployment successful!"
          echo "Version: ${{ github.sha }}"
          echo "Deployed at: $(date -u +'%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ')"
          # Example: Send Slack notification
          # curl -X POST -H 'Content-type: application/json' \
          #   --data '{"text":"✅ Production deployment successful! Commit: ${{ github.sha }} by ${{ github.actor }}"}' \
          #   ${{ secrets.SLACK_WEBHOOK_URL }}

      - name: Notify deployment failure
        if: failure()
        run: |
          echo "❌ Production deployment failed!"
          echo "Version: ${{ github.sha }}"
          echo "Please investigate immediately."
          # Example: Send Slack alert
          # curl -X POST -H 'Content-type: application/json' \
          #   --data '{"text":"❌ Production deployment FAILED! Commit: ${{ github.sha }} by ${{ github.actor }}. Investigate immediately!"}' \
          #   ${{ secrets.SLACK_WEBHOOK_URL }}

  rollback:
    name: Automatic Rollback on Failure
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    needs: deploy
    if: failure()
    steps:
      - name: Configure deployment credentials
        uses: aws-actions/configure-aws-credentials@v4
        with:
          aws-access-key-id: ${{ secrets.AWS_ACCESS_KEY_ID }}
          aws-secret-access-key: ${{ secrets.AWS_SECRET_ACCESS_KEY }}
          aws-region: ${{ secrets.AWS_REGION }}

      - name: Rollback to previous version
        run: |
          echo "⚠️ Initiating automatic rollback..."
          echo "Failed commit: ${{ github.sha }}"
          # Example rollback strategy: restore previous S3 version
          # aws s3 sync s3://${{ secrets.PRODUCTION_BACKUP_BUCKET }} s3://${{ secrets.PRODUCTION_S3_BUCKET }} --delete
          # aws cloudfront create-invalidation --distribution-id ${{ secrets.CLOUDFRONT_DISTRIBUTION_ID }} --paths "/*"
          echo "Rollback completed. Please verify production stability."
